old_claim.note: Food Storage Pits provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Earlier bins and pits form the storage lineage from which purpose-built raised-floor granaries emerged.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and Figure 2 caption: identify purpose-built granaries at Dhra' around 11,300-11,175 cal BP, before plant domestication, with suspended wooden floors and mud walls.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Dhra' had purpose-built predomestication granaries with suspended floors and mud walls around 11,300-11,175 cal BP.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The chronology supports predecessor lineage rather than a one-to-one enabling dependency. Purpose-built raised-floor granaries extend earlier food-storage systems but do not technically require a pit.

ontology_before: food_storage_pits was modeled as an enabling capability for granaries.
ontology_after: food_storage_pits is retained as historical lineage for granaries, not as a necessary implementation capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: granaries->food_storage_pits is historical_predecessor.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that the scoped granaries implementation technically requires food_storage_pits.
short_reason: Purpose-built raised-floor granaries extend earlier food-storage systems but do not technically require a pit.
